---
title: "Monoid"
generated: true
description: "Associative reduction with identity, carrying optional pytree state."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.semiring`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.semiring._types`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Monoid -->
```python
class Monoid(Protocol[S])
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:Monoid -->
Associative reduction with identity, carrying optional pytree state.

A monoid supplies the additive `(+)` structure of a semiring: an
identity element and an associative combine over an accumulator that
may itself be an arbitrary pytree of auxiliary state.

Required (informal) properties:

- `init` returns the identity element broadcast to `shape`.
  The returned object is the same pytree shape used by `update`
  and `merge`.
- `update` folds a single value into the accumulator. Used
  inside the K loop of the streaming kernels.
- `merge` is associative and has `init` as identity. Used
  between fully-reduced sub-blocks (e.g. when joining results across
  Pallas blocks, or in cross-device tree reductions). `merge`
  and `update` must agree: `update(acc, v)` must equal
  `merge(acc, update(init, v))`.
- `finalize` projects the (possibly auxiliary) state to the
  user-facing array. Applied once at the end of the contraction.

The state `S` may be any pytree; this is what enables online
numerically-stable reductions such as the `(running_max, sum_exp)`
state used by a log-sum-exp monoid.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Monoid.init -->
```python
init(shape: tuple[int, ...], dtype: jnp.dtype[Any]) -> S
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Return the identity element broadcast to `shape`.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Monoid.update -->
```python
update(acc: S, value: Num[Array, '*shape']) -> S
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Fold a single value into the accumulator.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Monoid.merge -->
```python
merge(a: S, b: S) -> S
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Combine two fully-reduced accumulator states.

Associative, with `init` as identity, so that partially
reduced sub-blocks may be recombined in any order.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Monoid.finalize -->
```python
finalize(acc: S) -> Num[Array, '*shape']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Project the accumulator state to the user-facing array.

Applied once at the end of the contraction to discard any
auxiliary state (such as a running maximum) and return the
reduced value.


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:Monoid -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*shape` | a variadic group of axes (`shape`) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
